summary: Excellent soundboard capture of an underrated May 3 1972 show, highlighted by a marathon Truckin > Other One jam and tight playing throughout.
review: Reviewers rave about the phenomenal extended sequence that runs from Truckin through Other One, Drums, Me & Bobby McGee and ends on Wharf Rat, calling the performance “phenomenal” and “extremely well played.” The soundboard recording is described as crystal‑clear and excellent, making this underrated show a must‑listen for Deadheads.

ai_rating.rationale: All five reviews are 4‑5 stars and praise both the musical performance and the clear soundboard recording; no significant negatives were noted.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear soundboard capture with no reported drop‑outs or muddiness; reviewers explicitly call the recording ‘excellent sounding.’

standout_songs: Truckin > Other One > Drums > Me and Bobby McGee > Wharf Rat
show_quality.setlist_flow: The show flows through a massive improvisational suite that weaves multiple songs together, delivering high energy and tight transitions; reviewers consider the May 3 performance underrated yet essential.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His wah‑wah tone and jam style are praised, likened to his playing on the Ace of Cups sessions.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum segment is highlighted as part of the extended jam sequence.
